title: Root Cli Interface Authority Audit
goal: Resolve #410 by making root CLI runtime ownership and remaining interface authority visible through a compact contract-backed audit.
non_goals: Do not remove Python runtime behavior.
Do not rewrite all CLI dispatch.
Do not block urgent runtime fixes.

intent.outcome: Root CLI authority is classified by legitimate runtime ownership versus extractable or guardable interface authority.
intent.constraints: Prefer contract inventory and checks over broad refactors.
intent.latitude: Expose the audit through an existing compact command or contract check if that gives future agents enough routing clarity.
intent.escalation: Escalate before changing generated command semantics or runtime primitive ownership.

execution.next_step: Archive #410 and continue #411 under the architecture-boundary lane.
execution.proof: Run focused contract/defaults tests, contract surface check, proof selector, summary, reconcile, and doctor.

invariants: Runtime primitive behavior remains Python-owned where live inspection or mutation sequencing is required.
Command/interface semantics route to contracts, IR, generated adapters, or explicit follow-on candidates.
The audit must name concrete next extraction or guard candidates.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Strengthen architecture boundaries after contract and generated-layer expansion.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After #410 closes, continue #411 and then validate the parent architecture-boundary lane.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Root CLI authority is now queryable through `agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json`, backed by python_runtime_boundary contract/schema data and enforced by contract tooling checks.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: #411 and the parent architecture-boundary lane remain open.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Package proof correctly flagged the direct `src/agentic_workspace/cli.py` edit for review. The edit is accepted as thin projection glue over contract-owned audit data, not as new command/interface authority in cli.py.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for #410; #411 remains for source-to-payload-to-installed synchronization proof.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Ingest and implement all issues lane by lane. Commit and push after each milestone. Collect dogfooding evidence.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue the architecture-boundary lane by landing #410 with checked-in proof and continuation evidence.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Implement #410 as a contract-backed root CLI authority audit and compact query.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the audit distinguishes runtime primitive ownership from interface authority and names next candidates.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: python runtime boundary contract/schema, contract checks/tests, compact defaults output, planning evidence surfaces
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused tests, contract surface check, proof selector, summary, reconcile, doctor
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any runtime primitive extraction or generated adapter semantic change.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated outputs unless a generator/check requires them.

context_budget.live working set: issue #410, python_runtime_boundary contract, contract checks, defaults section tests
context_budget.recoverable later: GitHub issue #410 and the parent architecture-boundary lane body
context_budget.externalize before shift: Keep this plan updated with proof and closeout state.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Active #410: add contract-backed root CLI authority audit.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Need for broad CLI refactor or runtime primitive extraction.

references.label: Track and reduce root CLI interface authority

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Make root CLI interface authority visible and reducible.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: No broad refactor, no runtime primitive removal, and no generated command semantic change.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: The exact contract field and compact query shape.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The fix requires moving runtime behavior or changing command semantics.

immediate_next_action: Extend python_runtime_boundary with root CLI authority audit and tests.
invariants: Runtime primitive behavior remains Python-owned where live inspection or mutation sequencing is required.
Command/interface semantics route to contracts, IR, generated adapters, or explicit follow-on candidates.
The audit must name concrete next extraction or guard candidates.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "python_runtime_boundary or root_cli_authority"
uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "root_cli_authority or direct_cli_edit"

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/contracts/python_runtime_boundary.json src/agentic_workspace/contracts/schemas/python_runtime_boundary.schema.json src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/root-cli-interface-authority-audit.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: The repo can see which root CLI responsibilities are legitimate runtime ownership and which are remaining interface authority.
At least one next extraction or guard candidate is named.
Future direct CLI edits have clearer review routing.
Contract validation covers the audit shape.

execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q -k "python_runtime_boundary or root_cli_authority"; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "root_cli_authority or direct_cli_edit"; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json; uv run ruff check src tests scripts; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json; uv run agentic-workspace reconcile --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json

execution_run.next step: Archive #410, commit and push, close the GitHub issue, then promote #411.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Keep one owner per durable concern and make CLI implementation authority explicit rather than implicit in cli.py.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer contract-backed audit and check over runtime refactor.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can future direct CLI edits route through clearer authority classes?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #410 acceptance is satisfied, but the architecture-boundary lane remains open.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: python_runtime_boundary root_cli_authority_audit; defaults root_cli_authority output; contract tooling check; focused/full tests; proof/summary/reconcile/doctor.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #410 if root CLI authority changes without updating the contract audit or future direct cli.py edits cannot be classified by the audit.
